From experience, data management and clean-up is one of the core issues frustrating firms and Palantir's model goes beyond traditional analytics.

As you likely know, Palantir's journey started with the government-focused Gotham platform, but now the business is growing mostly in the commercial segment. Artificial Intelligence Platform - or AIP - is the engine behind this growth integrating LLM into the process.

Customer acquisition for new software would historically be one of the main hurdles as organizations don't like to undertake the investment, switching costs and user training. But, Palantir's boot camps with clients - where they showcase tailored solutions, using clients data - solved this problem and are boosting PLTR's growth.

All of that culminated to the point, where its shares are up 164% in a span of the last 12 months.

In Q3 2025, Palantir reported $1.18B in revenue, growth of 63% YoY, above management's guidance and analyst's.

The margins also stayed high, with adj. EBITDA at 51%.

The firm seems unstoppable, and the earnings reports it delivers are the best of any S&P 500 company.

Alex C. Karp, Co-Founder of Palantir, expressed his enthusiasm during the earning release:

114% - our Rule of 40 score! These results make undeniable the transformational impact of using AIP to compound AI leverage. Year-over-year growth in our U.S. business surged to 77%, and year-over-year growth in U.S. commercial climbed to 121%. We are yet again announcing the highest sequential quarterly revenue growth guide in our company’s history, representing 61% year-over-year growth.

The rule of 40 is a simple measure used among SaaS and growing tech firms - particularly those with recurring revenue - where if the sum of revenue growth and operating margin or free cash flow margin is above 40 it signals to investors a healthy balance between growth and profitability. PLTR's 114% is superb - in its own league. To put it into perspective, Nvidia's score back in January 2025 was 176 (now at 130%).

I don't want to dig deep into the Q3 earnings, because on February 3 we are already expecting Q4 earnings, but I just want to highlight a few key things which are important to my investment thesis.

One top thing is the switch from government-focused revenue to commercial. In Q3, government revenue still made-up 53.5% of the overall revenue, but the gap between the two is shrinking. As you can see commercial revenue grew 73% to $548M and government grew 55% to $633M. In the next 12–18 months, the commercial segment should take the lead in absolute terms.

Why is this so important?

I recall myself contemplating if I should buy Palantir's stock back in 2022 when the shares were trading in the range of $7-$10/share and I decided not to, as back then government revenue made-up most of the firm's revenue and the projects were secretive without enough publicly available data to estimate what the growth could be in the future of this segment and whether the revenue will be durable and scalable.

The commercial segment is much more straightforward, with a clear growth path. Also, we know what firms Palantir is working with and that the size of the market is roughly double the government market.

Unfortunately, I missed the boat, the market re-priced the stock and as I said earlier, it's too expensive for my risk profile now. But, that's the game of investing. It wasn't the first time, and it won't be the last.

For Q4 2025, Palantir is expecting $1.32-$1.33B of revenue, above the consensus of analysts of $1.19B. The commercial revenue growth is expected to be around ≈75%, well ahead of government ≈51%.

The EPS for the quarter are forecasted at $0.23/share and for the full-year 2025 at $0.72/share, marking 76% growth.

Investor's Takeaway

In summary, Palantir is on a hot streak. Reporting superb results, executing with excellence and investors can't get enough, rewarding the stock with massive gains in the last few years.

Palantir's state-of-the-art AIP platform is set to be the main growth driver in the next 1–3 years, as the TAM is estimated at double the government market. In the next 12-18 months, the commercial segment is set to overtake government-based revenue, which will mark a turning point for Palantir.

The company's execution is flawless with it's bootcamps to acquire new customers, showcasing the scalability of it's business model and the ease to acquire new customers, while solving business-critical issues.

Palantir's forward path is clear and I expect it to be very successful. The issue nonetheless is that the market is pricing in all of that, and then some. At a P/E of 244x it's the most expensive stock in our coverage and in the S&P 500 and the risk/reward isn't ideal.

data.title: Realty Income: My Best REIT Idea For 2026
data.description:
My Long Thesis For Realty Income

Realty Income (O) is a leading-retail focused real estate investment trust with improving geographic diversification, retail real estate category domination and adjusted funds from operations growth that has been realized consistently over long periods of time. Realty Income has a very moderate AFFO-derived dividend payout ratio and is increasingly focused on real estate markets outside of the United States.

The REIT is a reliable dividend machine, with a three-decade long history of paying dividends that makes shares attractive for you if you want to achieve monthly dividend income from a blue-chip real estate investment trust.

Retail-Dominated Asset Portfolio

Retail assets dominate in Realty Income’s property portfolio: approximately 82% all rental income that the real estate investment trust achieves comes from its leasing activity in the United States and approximately 80% of all cash flows come from high-quality retail properties.

Realty Income’s industry breakdown reveals a pronounced focus on needs-based shopping categories such as grocery stores, convenience stores, home improvement stores and drug stores, all of which ensure steady traffic to the properties of Realty Income’s tenants.

Probably the most compelling aspect of an investment in Reality Income is the stability of underlying cash flows that are derived from the real estate investment trust’s property portfolio.

These cash flows are achieved through long-duration, inflation-hedged rental contracts, which have effective lease terms of 10 years or more, that give the REIT long-term visibility for its income and cash flows: in Q3 2025 Realty Income had a portfolio occupancy rate of 98.3% which has been consistently, for two and a half decades, been well above the median occupancy rate of S&P 500 REITs.

Long rental contracts are a great asset for any real estate investors, no matter whether they are retail or institutional. Realty Income’s lease expiration schedule hints at very low cash flow risks, considering that 44.5% of all leases expire in 2034 or after that.

2026 Could Be A Year Of Growth For Realty Income

2026 could be shaping up to be a strong year for the REIT sector in general, and especially Realty Income, which is set to benefit from lower interest rates.

Interest rates are a key economic variable for real estate investment trusts as they typically rely on quite a bit of debt in order to stem new real estate acquisitions. Over time, Realty Income has proven to be a very astute purchaser of new real estate assets and consistently added new properties to its existing portfolio.

Mainly, the REIT’s cash flow growth comes from being able to stem larger acquisitions with higher values. In fact, Realty Income’s AFFO growth accelerated once the REIT started to do bigger acquisitions.

Realty Income has engineered respectable adjusted funds from operations growth in the last three decades, with the lever for this growth being a tendency to lean into larger acquisitions. The acquisition of Spirit Realty Capital, which added both retail and industrial properties to the REIT’s portfolio, can be credited with a rather steep increase in the business’ adjusted funds from operations.

Realty Income’s Q3 2025 AFFO trickled in at $994 million, up 8.4% YoY, thanks to new properties added to the portfolio, contributing income and cash flow to Realty Income. The REIT acquired SRC in 2023 and may pursue new REIT acquisition opportunities in 2026, in my view.

Realty Income has been a prolific acquirer of primarily retail real estate, but it also broadened its strategic positioning by including industrial and gaming properties. In the first three quarters of 2025 Realty Income invested $3.9 billion into new income-generating real estate which is about as much as the real estate investment trust invested in the entire year of 2024.

Given Realty Income’s consistent track record of deploying cash into new real estate assets, I believe the REIT could be on track to at least invest $4-5 billion in 2026, with falling interest rates potentially making debt-financed acquisitions more attractive.

Fair Value Estimate And Game Plan

Realty Income’s updated outlook for 2025 sees $4.25 to $4.27 in adjusted funds from operations, which the business should easily achieve considering that the year-to-date total as of the third quarter was $3.19 (75% through to full year fiscal goal).

Realty Income has a 2026 Price-To-AFFO Ratio of 12.8 vs. 11.0 for National Retail Properties (NNN) and 12.5 for W.P. Carey (WPC). Both REIT peers hold either retail or industrial properties in their portfolios, or both.

With interest rates in all likelihood set to fall a lot more in 2026 and the U.S. economy roaring, my prediction is for strong AFFO performance this year for all three REITs. Hence, the AFFO multiples for O, NNN and WPC in the table below are derived from my prediction that all REITs will see roundabout 5% AFFO growth in 2026.

My game plan with respect to Realty Income is to buy shares of the real estate investment trust incrementally at prices between $56 and $58 (which is consistent with a 13.0 Price-To-AFFO Ratio that I consider fair for Realty Income given the consistency of its operational results over a long period of time).

Above $58 I see Realty Income as a hold and below $56 as a strong buy. Compared to other REITs, I am prepared to pay a small premium for Realty Income thanks to the long payment record dating back to the mid-90s.

Suitability, Risk Analysis

80% Retail Exposure: While retail assets tend to be low risk assets (as people need to shop for groceries and other retail products on a recurring basis), 80% category exposure limits upside potential from other real estate asset classes such as industrial or gaming.

Borrowing Costs And Interest Rates: As interest rates increase, Realty Income’s borrowing costs go up. If the Fed were to change course in 2026 and stall on rate cuts, the real estate investment trust may not be in a position to stem higher-value acquisitions. This would to some extent negate the bullish core thesis I am putting forth here.

My Verdict: Buy

Realty Income is a very good buy if you want to have monthly dividend income.

The REIT is one of the largest real estate investment franchises in the United States and has applied conservative management principles for decades. Realty Income is well managed, is growing its adjusted funds from operations, and has recently increased its geographic reach by investing more money outside of the United States, especially in Europe.

Realty Income has proven to be a highly dependable income machine for many decades, and the REIT has seen an acceleration in its AFFO growth in recent years that relates back to being able to pull off acquisitions with higher enterprise values.

data.title: AXT Expects Lower Revenues For Q4 2025, But 2026 Outlook Remains Strong
data.description:
AXT, Inc. (AXTI) revised down its expected Q4 2025 revenue last Thursday and its stock price fell around 22%. The price has since recovered. The company attributed the lower revenue target to delays in export permits from China for indium phosphide. AXT expects the permits to go through during Q1-2026.

AXT’s stock price has undergone a strong rally over the last three months because it is one of the only providers of indium phosphide substrate, which is in high demand for data centers and AI computing. The company’s stock price is up 979% over the last year. The price is up 171% since I covered the company last November on Seeking Alpha.

Currently, the stock price is overvalued. Investor sentiment remains strong on the company and other suppliers of infrastructure for data centers. Some traders feel that the AI sector has reached a bubble. Data centers will continue to need updated and many new facilities are being built to keep up with AI computing. I continue my rating of a Hold for the company.

Downward Revision for Q4-2025

AXT expected strong Q4 revenues between $27 million and $30 million. The company reported a backlog of $49 million in indium phosphide orders awaiting export permits. AXT says that the permits will be delayed and fulfilment will begin during Q1-2026. The company now expects Q4 earnings between $22.5 million and 23.5 million. Market consensus for Q4 revenues was $28.77 million.

The company expects higher revenues from indium phosphide in the later half of 2026. AXT foresees a long-term trend in demand for data center build outs. The company raised more capital through a public offering of shares to expand its manufacturing capabilities. It will expand manufacturing of indium phosphide substrates at subsidiary Tongmei Xtal Technology in Beijing.

Indium phosphide substrate is in demand from the building and updating of data center infrastructure. AI computing is driving the growth of data centers and new build-outs. Indium phosphide is also used in lasers and detectors for high-speed optical connectivity. AXT saw historically high revenues during Q3-2025 for indium phosphide.

The company will report its Q4 2025 financial results on February 16th.

Q3-2025 Results

AXT reported improved financial performance for Q3 2025. Revenue of $28 million represented a 55% increase QoQ and an 18% increase YoY. Gross profit was also higher. The company reported a gross profit of $6.2 million, representing a 343% increase QoQ and a 9% increase YoY.

Revenues from indium phosphide drove overall performance. Indium phosphide revenues increased 250% QoQ. The company attributed the increase to receiving export permits and fulfilling backlog.

Gallium arsenide is the company’s second highest revenue source. Revenues from gallium arsenide were $7.5 million, a 20% increase YoY. The third source of revenue comes from the company’s raw material subsidiary. Its revenues were $6.7 million. The company also reported $640K in revenues from sales of germanium substrates.

The company reported an operating loss of $384K versus a loss of $6.1 million QoQ and a loss of $2.6 million YoY. Net loss was $1.2 million versus a loss of $6.4 million QoQ and a net loss of $2.1 million YoY. Net loss and operating loss have improved and overall operating expenses have decreased. Operating expenses for Q3 were $6.7 million.

Cash and ST investments were $31.2 million. The amount will increase since the company raised capital. Total debt is $61.5 million. The company does not report free cash flow.

Although AXT downgraded its revenue expectations for Q4-2025, it expects revenues to increase in 2026 from indium phosphide backlog fulfillment. The company also expects its manufacturing capacity for indium phosphide to double during 2026.

The other companies specialize in hardware and software for data centers. The stock price for each company is currently overvalued due to positive investor sentiment and strong demand from data centers. It is unclear when the uptrend will subside.

Investment Strategy and Risk

AXT is at low risk of poor financial performance. Q4 revenues were downgraded, but the company has positive 2026 outlook. Its products remain in high demand and the company is increasing manufacturing capacity. The company’s assets outweigh its liabilities and debt is manageable. The company has plenty of liquidity to continue its current strategy. The only risk is the continued delay of export permits for indium phosphide.

A Hold rating for AXT comes at a moderate to high risk. The price is overvalued and Q4 earning may cause a new downtrend in stock price. Overall, it is likely that investor sentiment will remain strong for this sector due to high demand.

AXT foresees higher revenues during the later half of 2026. The company recently raised capital to increase its manufacturing capacity for indium phosphide. Financial performance has improved for the company and it is benefiting from increased demand for its products. I continue my rating of a Hold for the company.
